cases.title: Compact Expression May Stay Compact
cases.purpose: Show that readable rendering does not need to expand every expression.
invariants: The head Equal remains visible.
Argument order is unchanged.
The nested Get[...] call remains visible.
The display does not imply a different comparison rule from the canonical expression.

cases.title: Multiline Display Is Not Canonical Text
cases.purpose: Make the GE-002 conformance-suite theme concrete: canonical text and readable render are different layers.
cases.canonical_text: ForEach[Query[n_, Equal[Get[n_, "category"], "Person"]], p_, Do[Trace[Traverse[p_, "born_in"]], Update[p_, {"visited": True[]}]]]
cases.readable_render_candidate:
ForEach[
  Query[
    n_,
    Equal[
      Get[n_, "category"],
      "Person"
    ]
  ],
  p_,
  Do[
    Trace[Traverse[p_, "born_in"]],
    Update[p_, {"visited": True[]}]
  ]
]
invariants: The display is allowed to use newlines and indentation.
The display remains traceable to the same expression tree.
The display must not become the only stored representation.
A future renderer must not require transport consumers to parse this display text to recover machine data.

cases.title: Dictionaries Keep Stable Field Visibility
cases.purpose: Show that readable rendering must not hide dictionary fields or reorder them in an unstable way.
invariants: Both dictionary fields remain visible.
Field order is stable.
Boolean values remain explicit CogLang boolean expressions.
The display does not imply a host persistence result.

cases.title: Error Expressions Keep The Error Head
cases.purpose: Show that readable rendering of error values should preserve the error category and key arguments.
invariants: The error head remains visible.
Key arguments are not dropped or replaced by prose.
The display does not turn an error value into a host exception.
Additional UI annotations may be shown outside the render, but they must not overwrite the canonical error value.

cases.title: Transport Views Keep Structured Data Separate
cases.purpose: Show the expected split when a future host or UI wants to show both machine data and human-readable display.
cases.transport_expectation: If a future host-visible envelope carries a readable render, that render should be auxiliary display data. The envelope should still preserve structured fields and canonical text or a reference to canonical text.
invariants: The readable render is not the transport serialization.
Machine consumers should use structured envelope fields.
Human review can show readable render next to canonical text.
HRC v0.2 is not expanded by this example.
